seo.title: React (general) implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping React (general) projects safely.

content.intro: This production-readiness checklist provides a technical verification framework for React applications to ensure performance, accessibility, and reliability standards are met before deployment.

items.title: Implement Route-Based Code Splitting
items.description: Utilize React.lazy and Suspense to split the main bundle into route-specific chunks, reducing the initial JavaScript payload size.

items.title: Audit Component Re-renders
items.description: Use the React DevTools Profiler to identify and eliminate redundant renders in expensive components using React.memo or the React Compiler.

items.title: Optimize Image Assets
items.description: Verify all images use modern formats (WebP/AVIF), include explicit width/height to prevent Layout Shift, and implement lazy loading for off-screen content.

items.title: Analyze Bundle Composition
items.description: Run source-map-explorer or a similar bundle analyzer to identify and replace oversized or duplicate third-party dependencies.

items.title: Stabilize Hook Dependencies
items.description: Ensure all useEffect, useMemo, and useCallback hooks have complete dependency arrays to prevent stale closures or infinite loops.

items.title: Configure Cache Stale Times
items.description: Set explicit staleTime and gcTime in React Query or SWR to prevent excessive background refetches and unnecessary network traffic.

items.title: Implement Global Error Boundaries
items.description: Wrap the application root and high-risk feature modules in Error Boundaries to prevent a single component crash from unmounting the entire tree.

items.title: Validate Form States
items.description: Verify that all forms manage 'isSubmitting' and 'isValid' states to prevent double-submissions and provide immediate user feedback.

items.title: Sanitize Context Providers
items.description: Verify that Context providers only pass memoized values to avoid triggering re-renders in all consumer components when the provider's parent renders.

items.title: Handle Empty and Loading States
items.description: Ensure every data-driven component has defined skeleton screens or empty-state UI to prevent layout jumping during fetch operations.

items.title: Verify Keyboard Focus Management
items.description: Ensure all interactive elements are reachable via Tab and that focus is programmatically moved when opening or closing modals and drawers.

items.title: Audit ARIA Attributes
items.description: Verify that custom UI components (tabs, accordions, dropdowns) use correct aria-expanded, aria-controls, and role attributes for screen readers.

items.title: Check Color Contrast
items.description: Validate that all text-to-background combinations meet WCAG AA standards (minimum 4.5:1 ratio) using automated linting or browser tools.

items.title: Use Semantic HTML Elements
items.description: Replace non-semantic div/span click handlers with actual button or anchor tags to ensure native keyboard support and accessibility tree mapping.

items.title: Implement Skip Links
items.description: Add a hidden 'Skip to Main Content' link at the start of the DOM to allow keyboard users to bypass navigation menus.

items.title: Enforce Strict TypeScript
items.description: Enable strict mode in tsconfig.json and eliminate 'any' types to ensure type safety across component props and API responses.

items.title: Test Critical User Paths
items.description: Execute integration tests using React Testing Library for 'Happy Path' scenarios like user authentication and core transaction flows.

items.title: Mock API Responses for Edge Cases
items.description: Use Mock Service Worker (MSW) to simulate 404, 500, and timeout errors to verify UI resilience under failure conditions.

items.title: Configure Pre-commit Hooks
items.description: Utilize Husky to run ESLint and Prettier before every commit to ensure code consistency and prevent syntax errors in the repository.

items.title: Verify Environment Variable Isolation
items.description: Check that no sensitive server-side keys are prefixed with VITE_ or NEXT_PUBLIC_, which would expose them to the client-side bundle.

items.title: Sanitize Rendered HTML
items.description: Use DOMPurify when rendering strings through dangerouslySetInnerHTML to prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities.

items.title: Implement Content Security Policy (CSP)
items.description: Configure CSP headers to restrict script execution to trusted domains and block unauthorized inline scripts.

items.title: Audit Dependency Vulnerabilities
items.description: Run 'npm audit' or 'yarn audit' and resolve high-risk vulnerabilities in the node_modules tree.

items.title: Integrate Error Tracking
items.description: Connect a service like Sentry to capture unhandled exceptions and stack traces from production users for proactive debugging.

items.title: Remove Console Logs
items.description: Configure the build pipeline to strip console.log and console.debug statements from production builds to prevent information leakage.
